title: Map Electric Fields
description: Write a program to calculate and visualise electric fields from arbitrary charge configurations using Coulomb's law and the superposition principle.
whyItMatters: Electric fields are fundamental to electromagnetism. Visualising them builds deep intuition for how charges interact, how capacitors work, and how electromagnetic shielding operates — concepts essential for electronics and physics alike.

steps.title: Single point charge
steps.description: Calculate the electric field on a 2D grid from a single point charge. Plot the field using quiver plots (arrows) and streamlines.
steps.tips: Use meshgrid to create the grid. Be careful with the singularity at the charge location.

steps.title: Dipole field
steps.description: Place a positive and negative charge and calculate the combined field using superposition. Observe the dipole field pattern.
steps.tips: The dipole field falls off as 1/r³ far from the charges — much faster than a monopole.

steps.title: Equipotential lines
steps.description: Calculate the electric potential V and overlay contour plots of equipotential lines on the field map. Verify that field lines are perpendicular to equipotentials.
steps.tips: Use matplotlib contour for equipotentials and streamplot for field lines.

steps.title: Complex configurations
steps.description: Model a parallel-plate capacitor (rows of alternating charges), a quadrupole, and a conducting sphere (ring of charges). Compare to textbook field patterns.
steps.tips: A conducting sphere can be modelled as many equally spaced charges on a circle.

steps.title: Interactive exploration
steps.description: Add the ability to place charges interactively (click to add positive/negative charges) and update the field in real time.
steps.tips: Use matplotlib event handling or switch to Plotly for interactivity.

deliverables: Field visualisations for point charge, dipole, and capacitor
At least 4 different charge configurations visualised
Written discussion of field patterns and physical intuition gained

rubric.description: Coulomb's law and superposition correctly implemented
Clear, well-labelled field and equipotential plots
At least 4 distinct charge configurations explored
Written discussion demonstrates understanding of field behaviour
